body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
closes #356

Change how Y and p are created to make sure they always have the correct FieldVector structure.

This will allow us to use the most recent ClimaTimeSteppers :)

## To Do
- [x] open issue re: allocations introduced by `filter_nt` https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLSM.jl/issues/434

## Content
- [x] change `initialize_vars` to return `nothing` if component doesn't have prognostic/aux vars
- [x] add `filter_nt` function to remove key/value pairs containing `nothing` from a NamedTuple
- [x] call `filter_nt` from where `initialize_vars` is called for canopy models
- [x] add test to verify that `zero(Y)` can be called without error
- [x] remove ClimaTimeSteppers pin and use v0.7.15

## Notes/questions
- Is it okay to just use `filter_nt` in canopy case? `initialize_vars` change is made for all models so could require filtering for other model types too
- Canopy tests are kind of hard-coded to only check hydraulics - is this okay?
- We probably don't need to use `filter_nt` for `initialize_aux`, but it might be nice to be consistent between Y and p


<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: fix state FieldVec bug
